output.final_deliverable:
Collected links from Wikipedia Main Page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page)

Instructions followed: I used the visited Main Page content and the extracted link lists from the run. For each of 10 links below I give: (A) link URL, (B) link text/title (as shown on/near the Main Page), (C) a brief 1-2 sentence description of what that page is about, (D) verbatim evidence (quote) from the provided visit data showing the item was present on the Main Page, and (E) URL citations (Main Page and the link URL) for the factual claims.

Notes on evidence: verbatim quote lines come from the provided data (RAW VISIT CONTENT and RETRIEVED MEMORIES / MERGED RESULTS). Each entry includes at least one verbatim quote from the provided data showing the link text or related Main Page text.

1) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents
   Link text / title: Contents
   Description: The Contents page is Wikipedia's navigational hub listing content portals, indices, and ways to find and browse encyclopedia content. It helps users find articles by subject, portal, or index.
   Verbatim evidence: "- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents (Contents)" (from the collected links list in the run data)
   Citations: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page ; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents

2) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Current_events
   Link text / title: Current events (Portal:Current events)
   Description: The Current events portal gathers and summarizes recently occurring world news and ongoing events; it is Wikipedia's curated entry point for timely topics and breaking news.
   Verbatim evidence: "- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Current_events (Current events)" (from the collected links list in the run data) and the Main Page's "In the news" section which groups current-news items: "In the news" (from RAW VISIT CONTENT).
   Citations: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page ; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Current_events

3) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Random
   Link text / title: Special:Random (Random article)
   Description: Special:Random opens a randomly selected Wikipedia article each time it is used — a way to explore Wikipedia by surprise.
   Verbatim evidence: "- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Random (Random article)" (from the collected links list in the run data)
   Citations: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page ; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Random

4) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:About
   Link text / title: About Wikipedia
   Description: The About page explains what Wikipedia is (a free encyclopedia written by volunteers), how it works, and its mission and scope.
   Verbatim evidence: The Main Page opening text states: "Welcome to\nWikipedia\n,\nthe\nfree\nencyclopedia\nthat\nanyone can edit\n." (RAW VISIT CONTENT). Also: "- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:About (About Wikipedia)" (from the collected links list in the run data).
   Citations: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page ; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:About

5) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contact_us
   Link text / title: Contact Wikipedia
   Description: The Contact page provides information on ways to contact the Wikimedia Foundation and on-site points of contact for broader questions about Wikipedia and its projects.
   Verbatim evidence: "- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contact_us (Contact Wikipedia)" (from the collected links list in the run data) and the Main Page mentions the Wikimedia host organization: "Wikimedia Foundation, a non-profit organization that also hosts a range of other volunteer projects:" (RAW VISIT CONTENT).
   Citations: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page ; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contact_us

6) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Contents
   Link text / title: Help (Help:Contents)
   Description: The Help:Contents page is the main index for help pages — it links to how-to guides, editing help, and policy introductions for readers and editors.
   Verbatim evidence: "- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Contents (Help)" (from the collected links list in the run data) and Main Page text listing help resources: "Help desk\n– Ask questions about using or editing Wikipedia." (RAW VISIT CONTENT).
   Citations: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page ; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Contents

7) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Introduction
   Link text / title: Learn to edit (Help:Introduction)
   Description: Help:Introduction is an introductory how-to guide for new editors that explains basic editing tasks and how to contribute to Wikipedia.
   Verbatim evidence: "- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Introduction (Learn to edit)" (from the collected links list in the run data) and Main Page listing: "Teahouse\n– Ask basic questions about using or editing Wikipedia." (RAW VISIT CONTENT).
   Citations: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page ; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Introduction

8) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Community_portal
   Link text / title: Community portal
   Description: The Community portal is the central hub for editors, with resources, links, tasks, and announcements for the Wikipedia community and editor coordination.
   Verbatim evidence: The Main Page text under "Other areas of Wikipedia" includes: "– The central hub for editors, with resources, links, tasks, and announcements." (RAW VISIT CONTENT). Also: "- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Community_portal (Community portal)" (from the collected links list in the run data).
   Citations: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page ; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Community_portal

9) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:RecentChanges
   Link text / title: Recent changes (Special:RecentChanges)
   Description: The Recent changes page lists the most recent edits to Wikipedia so readers and editors can monitor ongoing activity across the site.
   Verbatim evidence: "- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:RecentChanges (Recent changes)" (from the collected links list in the run data) and the Main Page has an entry line for monitoring edits under "Other areas of Wikipedia": "Recent changes" (RAW VISIT CONTENT).
   Citations: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page ; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:RecentChanges

10) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:File_upload_wizard
    Link text / title: File upload wizard (Wikipedia:File upload wizard)
    Description: The File upload wizard page guides users through uploading media files (images, audio) to Wikimedia Commons via Wikipedia and explains upload policy and steps.
    Verbatim evidence: "- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:File_upload_wizard" appears in the collected links list (RETRIEVED MEMORIES / MERGED RESULTS) as part of the Main Page links; and the Main Page lists sister project "Commons\nFree media repository" showing the related media hosting: "Commons\nFree media repository" (RAW VISIT CONTENT).
    Citations: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page ; https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:File_upload_wizard

Caveats / notes about evidence and process:
- The search/visit stage returned a list of links extracted from the Main Page (see the run's merged results). Example extracted link items (verbatim) included lines such as: "- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents (Contents)", "- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Current_events (Current events)", "- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Random (Random article)", etc. These verbatim lines are included above as evidence for the presence of the links on the Main Page (these lines appear in the run data under the collected links output).
- The Main Page text that was returned by the visit is quoted verbatim in places above where relevant (for example, the opening: "Welcome to\nWikipedia\n,\nthe\nfree\nencyclopedia\nthat\nanyone can edit\n."). That same Main Page content also lists sections such as "In the news", "Other areas of Wikipedia", and the "Wikimedia Foundation / sister projects" list, which I quoted where they support the descriptions above.

If you want, I can (A) fetch each of the 10 linked pages to extract precise first-paragraph summaries (one-liners) directly from each page, or (B) pick a different set of 10 links from the Main Page and repeat the same extraction. Note: the run attempted visiting linked pages but one visit failed (see logs) — I can retry full page visits on request.

Haliotis jacnensis
, commonly known as the Jacna abalone, is a species of
sea snail
in the abalone family, Haliotidae. It is found in the western Pacific Ocean at depths between sea level and 50 metres (160 ft), around the coasts of American Samoa, Fiji, Guam, Indonesia, Japan, Micronesia, New Caledonia, Niue, the Northern Mariana Islands, the Philippines, the Solomon Islands, Tonga, Tuvalu and Vanuatu.
H. jacnensis
features an oblong–ovate shell that varies in size between 7 to 25 millimetres (0.28 to 0.98 in). The shell is reddish-orange with a silvery interior and features irregular scaly ridges. This picture shows five views of a
H. jacnensis
shell, 18 millimetres (0.71 in) in length, found on
Masbate Island
in the Philippines.

Haliotis jacnensis
, commonly known as the Jacna abalone, is a species of
sea snail
in the abalone family, Haliotidae. It is found in the western Pacific Ocean at depths between sea level and 50 metres (160 ft), around the coasts of American Samoa, Fiji, Guam, Indonesia, Japan, Micronesia, New Caledonia, Niue, the Northern Mariana Islands, the Philippines, the Solomon Islands, Tonga, Tuvalu and Vanuatu.
H. jacnensis
features an oblong–ovate shell that varies in size between 7 to 25 millimetres (0.28 to 0.98 in). The shell is reddish-orange with a silvery interior and features irregular scaly ridges. This picture shows five views of a
H. jacnensis
shell, 18 millimetres (0.71 in) in length, found on
Masbate Island
in the Philippines.

A
galaxy
is a massive system bound together by
gravity
that contains
stars
and
surrounding matter
,
stellar remnants
,
interstellar
matter, and
dark matter
, all orbiting a common
center of gravity
. Typical galaxies range from as few as 10
million stars up to
supergiants
with 100 trillion stars, but most of the mass is dark matter. The
Solar System
is in the
Milky Way
galaxy, whose nearest large neighbor, the
Andromeda Galaxy
, is about 750,000
parsecs
(2.4 million
light years
) away from Earth; the two galaxies dominate the
Local Group
. There are probably more than 200
billion galaxies in the
observable universe
. Most are 1,000 to 100,000 parsecs (about 3,000 to 300,000
ly
) in diameter and are often separated from their neighbors by millions of parsecs. There is evidence that
supermassive black holes
exist at the center of many, if not all, galaxies. The Milky Way, a
spiral galaxy
with a diameter of at least 26,800 parsecs (87,400 ly), has
such an object
at the
Galactic Center
.
